reasoning: As of November 9, 2025, Bitcoin's price is hovering around $109,000 to $114,000, having dipped to approximately $105,000 in late October. The resolution criteria requires a 1-minute candle low of $90,000 or below by December 31, 2025 on Binance. A drop to $90,000 from current levels would represent roughly an 18-20% correction. In the cryptocurrency market, 20-30% drawdowns are historically very common during overall macro uptrends, and flash crashes or sudden liquidations can result in deep wicks on 1-minute candles. With nearly two months remaining until the resolution date (around 52 days), there is ample time for such an 18% downside volatility event to occur, making the probability substantial but not guaranteed. A 35% probability reflects the base rate of typical Bitcoin intra-cycle corrections over a two-month horizon when near all-time highs.
